document.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Propagating Non Cúram Data For Cúram Express RulesPropagating Non Cúram Data For Cúram Express Rules Guide

root.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Propagating Non Cúram Data For Cúram Express RulesPropagating Non Cúram Data For Cúram Express Rules Guide
root.summary: Front matter and the main guide for Cúram 8.1.2. It explains how to create initial CER Rule Objects for rate table data loaded outside Cúram APIs, especially bulk-loaded production data. The guide is aimed at system administrators and covers when to run the batch process, how it differs from API-based automatic propagation, and that it must run before system startup or other processing. It notes the process applies only to rate tables and that custom CER storage needs custom handling. It identifies the RuleObjectPropagatorControl table flag used to suppress automatic startup propagation, and the batch program name curam.core.sl.infrastructure.rate.intf.RateCreateInitialRuleObjects.propa with optional rateTableType parameter for all or specific rate tables. The remaining pages are notices, copyright, privacy policy, and trademarks.

children.title: Note

This section is mainly front matter and a table of contents previewing the guide “Propagating non Cúram Data for Cúram Express Rules,” which explains initial CER rule object creation for bulk-loaded rate table data, audience, related reading, and batch program setup/process details.

children.title: Contents

children.title: 1 Propagating non Cúram Data for Cúram Express Rules
1.1 Introduction
About This Guide
children.summary: Introduces this guide for system administrators on running the batch program that stores initial CER Rule Objects for rate data loaded outside application APIs. Explains that API-published rate table data automatically creates, updates, or removes stored CER Rule Objects via configured propagators. Also lists related references: the Cúram Express Rules Reference Manual and Inside Cúram Eligibility and Entitlement Using Cúram Express Rules, which cover rules, the Dependency Manager, and rule object propagation.

children.title: Audience
children.summary: Intended for system administrators involved in deploying and loading a new Cúram system. This section explains how to run the batch program that stores initial CER rule objects for rate data loaded outside application APIs, where API-published rate table data would otherwise create, update, or remove rule objects automatically. It also points to related documentation on Cúram Express Rules, the Dependency Manager, and rule object propagation.

children.title: Related Reading
children.summary: Lists related documentation for the guide on propagating non-Cúram data into Cúram Express Rules. It points readers to the Cúram Express Rules Reference Manual for full details on CER rules and the Dependency Manager, and to Inside Cúram Eligibility and Entitlement Using Cúram Express Rules for Rule Object Propagation. The section is mainly a navigation aid for background and deeper technical reference.

Introduces the guide for system administrators who need to run the batch program that stores initial CER Rule Objects for rate data loaded outside application APIs. Notes that API-published rate table data automatically creates, updates, or removes stored CER Rule Objects via CER Rule Object Propagators. Points to related references: the Cúram Express Rules Reference Manual for CER rules and the Dependency Manager, and Inside Cúram Eligibility and Entitlement Using Cúram Express Rules for Rule Object Propagation.

children.title: 1.2 Process Overview
Introduction
children.summary: Introduces a guide for system administrators on running a batch program to store initial CER Rule Objects for rate data loaded outside application APIs. Explains that API-published rate table data automatically creates, updates, or removes CER Rule Objects via configurable propagators. Also states the guide’s audience and points to related Cúram Express Rules documentation, including the Reference Manual and a guide to Rule Object Propagation.

children.title: Process Details
Initial Setup
The RuleObjectPropagatorControl Database Table
children.summary: This section explains the RuleObjectPropagatorControl database table, which holds a single row indicating whether initial CER rule object propagation has been executed. Setting the executed attribute to 1/true prevents automatic CER Rule Object creation on system startup when the batch program runs. It also introduces the rate table rule object propagator batch program, which can process all rate tables at once or one table at a time, and notes it must be run after bulk-loading rate table data outside the application APIs.

Explains initial setup for bulk-populated rate table data that was created outside application APIs and therefore lacks CER Rule Objects. It describes the RuleObjectPropagatorControl table, whose single row’s executed flag should be set to 1/true so startup does not try to create initial rule objects again after the batch run. It also introduces the rate table rule object propagator batch program, which can process all rate tables or one at a time, and must be run before system startup and after each bulk data load.

children.title: Running the Rate Table Rule Object Propagator Batch Program
Name
children.summary: This section identifies the batch process name as curam.core.sl.infrastructure.rate.intf.RateCreateInitialRuleObjects.propa and states that it accepts one parameter: rateTableType, used to specify the rate table to process or left blank to process all rate tables.

children.title: Parameters
children.summary: Lists the Parameters for the RateCreateInitialRuleObjects batch program. It notes the batch name curam.core.sl.infrastructure.rate.intf.RateCreateInitialRuleObjects.propa and describes a single parameter: rateTableType, which selects the rate table to process or can be left blank to process all rate tables. The rest of the pages are legal notices, copyright/license terms, privacy policy, and trademarks.

Describes how to run the Rate Table Rule Object Propagator batch program to create initial CER Rule Objects for bulk-loaded rate table data. It explains that the batch should be run before system startup, either once for all rate tables or separately per rate table, and that it only processes newly populated data. The batch name is curam.core.sl.infrastructure.rate.intf.RateCreateInitialRuleObjects.propa, with one parameter, rateTableType, which can be blank to process all rate tables. Remaining pages are legal notices and trademarks.

Explains how to propagate initial CER Rule Objects for bulk-loaded rate table data created outside application APIs. Covers when the batch must be run (before system startup and after each bulk load), that it processes only newly loaded data, and the RuleObjectPropagatorControl table flag that should be set to prevent startup from recreating objects. Gives the batch name curam.core.sl.infrastructure.rate.intf.RateCreateInitialRuleObjects.propa and its optional rateTableType parameter for all or specific rate tables. Remaining pages are legal notices and trademarks.

Explains how to create initial CER Rule Objects for rate data loaded outside application APIs, mainly for bulk-loaded rate tables. It contrasts API-based propagation with this batch-based approach, notes the batch must run before startup and after each new bulk load, and only processes newly added data. Describes the RuleObjectPropagatorControl flag to prevent automatic startup creation, and gives the batch name curam.core.sl.infrastructure.rate.intf.RateCreateInitialRuleObjects.propa with optional rateTableType to target all or specific rate tables.

Explains how to create initial CER Rule Objects for rate data loaded outside Cúram APIs, mainly bulk-loaded rate tables. It contrasts API-based automatic propagation with a batch program required after each external data load and before startup, processing only newly added data. Notes this applies only to rate tables; custom rule-object storage needs custom processing. Covers the RuleObjectPropagatorControl flag to suppress automatic startup creation and names the batch curam.core.sl.infrastructure.rate.intf.RateCreateInitialRuleObjects.propa with optional rateTableType to target all or specific tables.
